column2: Little/no recon; target picked from visible opportunity.
column4: May bring mismatched ammo or insufficient tools.
column5: Panics after leader injury, casualties, lights, or failed breach.
column6: Grabs nearest visible supplies; poor prioritization.
column7: Lights, walls, warnings, and one strong chokepoint often break them.

column2: Basic scouting; targets pantry, armory, or weak wall.
column3: Uses one flank or distraction; retreats if objective fails.
column4: Mostly correct ammo/tools; limited breaching kit.
column5: Will withdraw after 20-35% losses unless desperate.
column7: Patrol coverage, distributed racks, and safe rooms matter.

column2: Reads power instability, patrol gaps, weather, and recent losses.
column4: Correct loadouts; specialists carry cutters/power injectors.
column5: Stays calm; retreats on objective complete, not ego.
column7: Layered defense, counter-scouting, morale, and route control.

column2: May ignore normal survival math for ideology/site objective.
column3: Uses D hazards, captives, relays, or self-sacrifice.
column5: Does not break normally; can collapse if belief/relay disrupted.
column7: Disrupt leader, signal, or site state; avoid pure attrition fight.
